title: Einstein World Models
abstract: arXiv:2606.26969v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Does intelligence require the ability to reason about phenomena beyond direct experience? It is natural to suspect that some complex thought cannot be captured through language alone. However, of particular concern to this work, is whether visualising counterfactual events can complement language as a mechanism for complex thought. We ask whether LLMs can be trained to utilise such visualisation mechanisms, in a way that benefits their reasoning abilities. Motivated by this question, we propose Einstein World Models. EWMs are a blueprint for LLM-based reasoning systems that place visual-temporal rollouts inside the reasoning trace, allowing them to reason in ways that text alone may not support well. In an EWM, the LLM calls a world-module (not to be confused with a world model), to produce short rollouts of scenes under consideration. The returned rollout is treated not as the answer, but as an inspectable hypothesis that can support la

Title: Einstein World Models
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26969v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Does intelligence require the ability to reason about phenomena beyond direct experience? It is natural to suspect that some complex thought cannot be captured through language alone. However, of particular concern to this work, is whether visualising counterfactual events can complement language as a mechanism for complex thought. We ask whether LLMs can be trained to utilise such visualisation mechanisms, in a way that benefits their reasoning abilities. Motivated by this question, we propose Einstein World Models. EWMs are a blueprint for LLM-based reasoning systems that place visual-temporal rollouts inside the reasoning trace, allowing them to reason in ways that text alone may not support well. In an EWM, the LLM calls a world-module (not to be confused with a world model), to produce short rollouts of scenes under consideration. translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26969v1 公告类型：新 摘要：智能是否需要具备推理超出直接体验的现象的能力？人们自然会怀疑，某些复杂思维无法仅通过语言来捕捉。然而，本工作特别关注的是，可视化反事实事件是否能够作为复杂思维的一种机制来补充语言。我们询问大型语言模型是否可以被训练利用这种可视化机制，从而提升其推理能力。受此问题启发，我们提出了爱因斯坦世界模型（EWM）。EWM是一种基于大型语言模型的推理系统蓝图，它将视觉-时间推演置于推理轨迹内部，使得模型能够以纯文本可能无法很好支持的方式进行推理。在EWM中，大型语言模型调用一个世界模块（不要与世界模型混淆），生成所考虑场景的简短推演。返回的推演不被视为答案，而是作为一种可检查的假设，以支持后续推理。
tldr: 提出爱因斯坦世界模型（EWM），通过在推理轨迹中嵌入视觉-时间推演，使大型语言模型能够利用可视化反事实事件来增强复杂推理能力。
en_tdlr: Proposes Einstein World Models (EWMs), which embed visual-temporal rollouts within the reasoning trace, enabling LLMs to leverage visualization of counterfactual events to enhance complex reasoning.
